title: ranunculus acris var. friesianus
intro: Ranunculus acris var. friesianus, also known as Friesian buttercup, is a variety of the plant Ranunculus acris, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ranunculus acris var. friesianus include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a popular natural remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to soothe and calm irritated skin, or internally to support digestive health.
